verses.text: Though Moses and Samuel interceded, God says His heart could not turn toward this people; they must go forth to sword and captivity. Even great intercessors could not avail. There is a point where settled sin makes judgment sure.

Though Moses and Samuel interceded, God says His heart could not turn toward this people; they must go forth to sword and captivity. Even great intercessors could not avail. There is a point where settled sin makes judgment sure.

Though Moses and Samuel interceded, God says His heart could not turn toward this people; they must go forth to sword and captivity. Even great intercessors could not avail. There is a point where settled sin makes judgment sure.

Though Moses and Samuel interceded, God says His heart could not turn toward this people; they must go forth to sword and captivity. Even great intercessors could not avail. There is a point where settled sin makes judgment sure.
Who shall pity Jerusalem? God will winnow and bereave them, for they would not return. Sympathy is exhausted. A people who refuse to repent leave God no choice but to scatter them.
Who shall pity Jerusalem? God will winnow and bereave them, for they would not return. Sympathy is exhausted. A people who refuse to repent leave God no choice but to scatter them.
Who shall pity Jerusalem? God will winnow and bereave them, for they would not return. Sympathy is exhausted. A people who refuse to repent leave God no choice but to scatter them.
Their widows multiply, and 'she that hath borne seven languisheth.' Sorrow fills the homes. The losses of war reach into every family.
Their widows multiply, and 'she that hath borne seven languisheth.' Sorrow fills the homes. The losses of war reach into every family.

Jeremiah laments being born 'a man of strife,' hated though he wronged none in lending; yet God will deal well with the remnant. He feels the loneliness of his calling. The faithful servant is often opposed for no real fault.

Jeremiah laments being born 'a man of strife,' hated though he wronged none in lending; yet God will deal well with the remnant. He feels the loneliness of his calling. The faithful servant is often opposed for no real fault.

God will give their wealth to the spoil and make them serve their enemies in an unknown land, for their sins. Exile is decreed. The treasures they trusted become the plunder of their foes.
God will give their wealth to the spoil and make them serve their enemies in an unknown land, for their sins. Exile is decreed. The treasures they trusted become the plunder of their foes.

Jeremiah recalls, 'Thy words were found, and I did eat them; and thy word was unto me the joy... of mine heart,' yet asks why his pain is endless. He fed on God's word with delight. Even the soul that loves Scripture may wrestle with prolonged grief.

God answers: 'If thou return, then will I bring thee again'; let the people turn to him, but he must not turn to them; God will make him a brazen wall and deliver him. Restoration follows return. The wavering servant is recalled to firmness and promised God's help.
